title: RemoteMessenger Initialization Flow
code:
flowchart
	postMessage1(["postMessage({ kind: RemoteReady, ... })"])
	rm1--1-->postMessage1--2-->rm2
	subgraph MainProcess
		rm1["m1 = RemoteMessenger< MainProcessApi,WebViewApi >"]
	end
	subgraph WebView
		rm2["RemoteMessenger< WebViewApi,MainProcessApi >"]
	end

ai.summary: Illustrates the initial communication flow between MainProcess and WebView using RemoteMessenger for readiness signaling.
ai.whatItShows: This diagram depicts the initial handshake mechanism for RemoteMessenger between a MainProcess and a WebView. It specifically shows rm1 in the Main Process sending a RemoteReady message via postMessage to rm2 in the WebView, initiating communication.
ai.whenToUse: Use this pattern when establishing secure and synchronized communication channels between different isolated processes or contexts, such as a main application process and an embedded web view, ensuring both sides are ready before exchanging application-specific data.
ai.howToAdapt: This pattern can be adapted by adding acknowledgments, error handling, or versioning to the RemoteReady message. For more complex setups, consider a dedicated message broker or a more robust RPC mechanism. The RemoteMessenger can be extended to support various message kinds for different functionalities.
